name: Applied Aerospace & Defense, Inc.
business: Applied Aerospace & Defense, Inc. provides advanced design, engineering, and vertically integrated manufacturing solutions for mission-critical, highly engineered space and defense systems. Its products serve commercial, civil, and national security programs, supporting leading and next-generation space and defense technology companies. The company operates through a nationwide network of IP-enabled advanced manufacturing facilities.
sellsTo: Defense Aviation and Airborne Systems customers
C5ISR and Precision Strike Systems customers

exposures.sentence: Space and Launch Systems is one of our largest and fastest-growing end markets.

We are closely aligned with the programs driving space superiority, resilient national security architectures, and the modernization of the defense industrial base, positioning us alongside customers executing the most critical, well-funded missions and growing programs.

Modernization efforts that emphasize persistent surveillance are driving increased demand for advanced radar, RF, and electro-optical/infrared (EO/IR) sensing systems deployed across ground, airborne, maritime, and space-based platforms to enable next-generation situational awareness.
The space systems market is expanding rapidly as satellite deployments accelerate across commercial communications, Earth-observation, and exploration missions.
Defense Aviation and Airborne Systems consists of manned and unmanned fixed-wing aircraft and rotorcraft.

Technology failures, cybersecurity breaches and other unauthorized access to or use of our information technology systems or sensitive or proprietary information could have a material adverse effect on our business and operations.

tamClaims.claim: The space economy will reach $1.8 trillion by 2035, nearly three times its $630 billion size in 2023.
tamClaims.sentence: The World Economic Forum projects that the space economy will reach $1.8 trillion by 2035, nearly three times its $630 billion size in 2023.

tamClaims.claim: More than 15,000 new on-orbit assets are planned by 2028.
tamClaims.sentence: More than 15,000 new on-orbit assets are planned by 2028 according to The World Economic Forum
